RMBC 2: Copywriting and Direct Response
in 2025
Welcome to RMBC 2, where we'll explore the reimagined RMBC method and the state of direct response in 2025. This
document is designed to be both beginner-friendly for those new to copywriting and valuable for seasoned veterans who can
benefit from reminders and unique insights about modern copywriting approaches.

-- 1 of 8 --

What is Copywriting?
At its core, copywriting is simply the art and science of
writing advertisements. However, in RMBC 2, we recognize
that copywriting doesn't exist in a vacuum - it's part of a
holistic marketing strategy.
This is a key difference from the original RMBC. Since 2020,
I've scaled more businesses, worked with thousands of
copywriters and hundreds of business owners, and
discovered that effective copywriting must be integrated
with the entire customer journey.
Many freelance and in-house copywriters think their job is
just to write emails, video sales letters, sales pages, or
advertorials. But that limited view doesn't work anymore.
You need to understand the holistic picture of how your
copy fits into the broader marketing strategy.
Direct Response Copywriting
Writing ads and marketing materials designed to get a
prospective customer to take an immediate,
measurable action (clicking a link, buying a product,
sharing a post, signing up for a newsletter, etc.)
Effectiveness Criterion
The sole criterion by which we judge an ad's
effectiveness is its ability to get the right kind of people
to consistently take the desired action when they see it.

-- 2 of 8 --

Two Crucial Customer Journeys
Journey 1: Prospect to Purchaser
This is the primary journey we'll focus on - moving
someone from point A (not a customer) to point B
(becoming a paying customer) through your copywriting
efforts.
Journey 2: Customer to Repeat
Purchaser
This journey focuses on getting existing customers to buy
again through upsells, email marketing, SMS, calls, and
other follow-up methods over weeks, months, or years.
The Sales Funnel Visualization
The first journey is often visualized as a sales funnel following the classic AIDA framework:
Awareness
A prospect becomes aware of your brand, product, or
service through an advertisement, organic discovery, or
recommendation.
Interest
The product generates their interest through curiosity,
emotional connection, or by speaking to their pain point,
causing them to click the ad.
Desire
On your website, they're sold even more as their desire
increases - not just for the product, but to solve their pain
point.
Action
They take action by purchasing your product or service.

-- 3 of 8 --

The Digital Advertising Landscape
The digital landscape for advertising is vast, with numerous platforms where customers might encounter your ads:
Social Media
Facebook, Instagram, and TikTok ads reach users
while they're connecting with friends and consuming
content.
Video Platforms
YouTube and other video platforms where users are
seeking education or entertainment.
Native Advertising
Ads that blend into content on news sites and other
publications.
E-commerce
In-app advertising on platforms like Shopify and
Amazon.
Email Marketing
Direct communication with prospects and customers
through their inbox.
Traditional Media
Radio and TV still play a role in the modern
advertising mix.
What's crucial to understand is that ads will typically look, feel, and be written differently depending on the platform. RMBC 2
will explore these platform-specific strategies in depth.

-- 4 of 8 --

Context is King: Platform-Specific
Strategies
The truth about direct response marketing is that context is incredibly important. This applies to all types of marketing that
elicit a direct response - from branded e-commerce to more aggressive approaches.
YouTube Example
People go to YouTube primarily for education or
entertainment. Therefore:
YouTube ads are typically video format (congruent with
the platform)
Educational or entertaining video ads outperform hard-
sell approaches
After clicking, users often go to a page featuring another
video
This maintains congruence as they're already in "video
watching mode"
Native Advertising Example
People encountering native ads are typically reading articles
online. Therefore:
Native ads are designed to look like links to interesting
news stories
They blend into surrounding content on news sites
After clicking, users typically go to an advertorial
Advertorials are crafted to look and feel like editorial
pieces or news stories
This maintains congruence with the reading experience
they were already having
Congruence is maintaining a consistent feeling and experience for a prospect throughout their journey from point A to point B.
Breaking this congruence can disrupt the customer journey and reduce conversion rates.

-- 5 of 8 --

Product Market Awareness: The
Foundation of Effective Advertising
Beyond platform context, product market awareness is crucial for targeting the right people with your copy. This concept will
be explored in depth in the Research module, but here's a brief example:
If you're selling a coffee product that provides energy without the caffeine crash, you want to target coffee drinkers who
love coffee but dislike the negative effects - not 8-year-olds or non-coffee drinkers. You want the right person to directly
respond to your ad.
The Complete Customer Journey
1	See Ad
Prospect encounters your ad on a platform where
they're already active
2 Click Ad
If the ad is effective, they click through to learn
more
3	Landing Experience
They arrive at a context-specific page (advertorial,
VSL, product page)
4 Add to Cart
They decide to purchase and add the product to
their cart
5	Checkout
They enter personal information, payment details,
and complete the purchase
Even at the checkout stage, copywriting plays a crucial role in getting customers to take that final step of entering their
information and clicking "place order."

-- 6 of 8 --

Sales Funnels in 2025
Marketing can seem complex in 2025 with all the different components and how they can be combined. However, when we
zoom out, there are several common patterns that work:
1 Classic Model
Ad ³ VSL ³ Checkout
This traditional approach can still work in certain
contexts.
2 Advertorial Path
Ad ³ Advertorial ³ VSL ³ Checkout
Using an advertorial as a bridge between the ad and
the sales message.
3 Product Page Integration
Ad ³ Advertorial ³ VSL ³ Product Page ³
Checkout
This is one of the most common approaches used by
8-figure businesses.
4 Direct to Product
Ad ³ Product Page ³ Checkout
A streamlined approach that works well for certain
products and audiences.
There's no single "right way" to sell. The optimal funnel depends on your specific product, audience, and platform. You'll need
to experiment and test different approaches to find what works best for your situation.
Many businesses that used to do well but are now struggling are still trying to make outdated models work. The reality is that
there are many different approaches that can be effective in 2025, and often the traditional video sales letter on a landing
page isn't the best choice anymore.

-- 7 of 8 --

The RMBC 2 Approach: Simplifying
Complexity and Leveraging AI
The Golden Age of Copywriting
Thanks to the rise of large language models (LLMs) and the specific processes you'll learn in RMBC 2, it's now easier than ever
to write great copy for all funnel assets in a fraction of the time it used to take.
2-4
High-Quality VSLs
Can be written in a single
day, compared to weeks in
the past
20-40
Good Ads
Can be created in a single
day with the right process
100+
Ad Photos
Can be generated in a day
with modern tools
1hr
Upsell Page
Can be completed in about
an hour (or even less with
practice)
This is a radical shift from the original RMBC in 2020, which was considered revolutionary for showing people how to write a
great sales letter in a week instead of eight weeks. Now, that same quality work can be done in hours rather than days.
Becoming a Copy-Thinker, Not Just a Copywriter
In this golden age where AI handles much of the heavy lifting, your knowledge, strategic thinking, and confidence as a
copywriting expert become the critical differentiating factors. RMBC 2 will transform you from a copywriter into a "copy-
thinker" - someone who can:
Understand the theory, structure, and essential elements of effective copy
Discern what makes good copy versus mediocre or bad copy
Generate endless high-quality copy using AI tools and specific processes
Think and edit like a top-tier copywriter
Apply strategic thinking to the entire marketing journey
RMBC 2 will help you think differently - like a marketer, like a strategist, like a copy-thinker. This shift in mindset, combined
with the practical tools and processes you'll learn, will revolutionize your approach to copywriting and marketing in 2025 and
beyond.
